items.final_wording: Supports clinician documentation of suspected or confirmed acute-COVID context, symptom duration from four weeks, fluctuating symptoms, initial consultation, functional change, further-assessment need and selected follow-up context without establishing diagnosis automatically.

source.title: COVID-19 rapid guideline: managing the long-term effects of COVID-19
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: Adults, children and young people with new or ongoing symptoms four weeks or more after suspected or confirmed acute COVID-19.
source.setting: Any healthcare setting, including primary and community care, with recommendation-specific remote, in-person and post-discharge qualifiers.
source.exclusions: Exact for long-COVID identification and holistic symptom review documentation; diagnosis, investigations, referral, management and UAE service pathways remain clinician-led and locally governed.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1 and 1.5–1.10 — case definition, initial consultation, assessment need, and follow-up context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician documentation of suspected or confirmed acute-COVID context, symptom duration from four weeks, fluctuating symptoms, initial consultation, functional change, further-assessment need and selected follow-up context without establishing diagnosis automatically.

items.rationale: Relevant only when suspected or confirmed acute-COVID history and the duration threshold are entered; it cannot attribute a generic sensory change to COVID.

items.final_wording: Supports recording acute-COVID history, current and previous symptom nature, severity, timing, duration, comorbidity, physical, cognitive, psychological, psychiatric and functional impact, and patient concerns without predicting course or generating management.

source.title: COVID-19 rapid guideline: managing the long-term effects of COVID-19
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: Adults, children and young people with new or ongoing symptoms four weeks or more after suspected or confirmed acute COVID-19.
source.setting: Any healthcare setting, including primary and community care, with recommendation-specific remote, in-person and post-discharge qualifiers.
source.exclusions: Exact for long-COVID identification and holistic symptom review documentation; diagnosis, investigations, referral, management and UAE service pathways remain clinician-led and locally governed.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording acute-COVID history, current and previous symptom nature, severity, timing, duration, comorbidity, physical, cognitive, psychological, psychiatric and functional impact, and patient concerns without predicting course or generating management.

items.rationale: Relevant to clinician-recorded symptom nature, timing, severity, comorbidity and function in post-COVID review without establishing cause or management.
